exercises.title: Hello Console
exercises.description: Use console.log to print Hello, World! to the console.
exercises.starterCode: // Print Hello, World! to the console

exercises.title: Variables & Sum
exercises.description: Declare two variables a = 10 and b = 20. Print their sum using console.log.
exercises.starterCode: // Declare a and b, then print the sum
exercises.hint: Declare with let a = 10; let b = 20; then console.log(a + b);

exercises.title: If-Else Statement
exercises.description: Write an if-else statement: if age = 18, print You can vote!, otherwise print Too young.
exercises.starterCode:
let age = 18;
// Write your if-else below

exercises.title: For Loop
exercises.description: Use a for loop to print numbers from 1 to 5, each on a new line.
exercises.starterCode: // Print numbers 1 to 5 using a for loop
exercises.hint: Use for (let i = 1; i <= 5; i++) { console.log(i); }

exercises.title: Function — Square
exercises.description: Create a function square(n) that returns n * n. Call it with 7 and print the result.
exercises.starterCode: // Create the square function and test it
exercises.hint: function square(n) { return n * n; } then console.log(square(7));

exercises.title: Array Methods
exercises.description: Create an array [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]. Use .filter() to get even numbers and print the result.
exercises.starterCode: // Filter even numbers from the array
exercises.hint: Use .filter(n => n % 2 === 0) and console.log the result.

exercises.title: Object & Properties
exercises.description: Create an object person with properties name: "Alice" and age: 25. Print Alice is 25 years old.
exercises.starterCode: // Create the person object and print the message
exercises.hint: Use template literals: `${person.name} is ${person.age} years old.`
exercises.solutionCode:
const person = { name: 'Alice', age: 25 };
console.log(`${person.name} is ${person.age} years old.`);

exercises.title: Arrow Functions & Map
exercises.description: Use an arrow function with .map() to double every number in [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]. Print the result.
exercises.starterCode: // Double every number using map and arrow function

exercises.title: Destructuring & Spread
exercises.description: Given const arr = [10, 20, 30], use destructuring to get the first two values into a and b. Use spread to create a new array [...arr, 40, 50]. Print both.
exercises.starterCode:
const arr = [10, 20, 30];
// Destructure and spread below

exercises.title: Promise & Async
exercises.description: Create a function fetchData() that returns a Promise resolving with "Data loaded!" after 100ms. Use async/await to call it and print the result.
exercises.starterCode: // Create fetchData with a Promise and use async/await
exercises.hint: return new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(() => resolve('Data loaded!'), 100));
